proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_009 == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `feature_009 == 0`.
The subgroup with `feature_009 == 1` and `feature_016 >= 0.964` has especially negative `dependency_BRAF` scores compared with all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_BRAF among feature_009 == 1 versus feature_009 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_009 == 1` lines had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.8047 versus -0.2495 in other lines, a difference of -0.5552.

analyses.code: Systematic subgroup screen over top BRAF modifiers; tested feature_009 == 1 and feature_016 >= 0.964 versus all other lines.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_009 == 1` and `feature_016 >= 0.964` subgroup had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.8286 versus -0.2965 in all other lines (n=1402), effect -0.5321. Pairwise interaction screening did not find a stronger, clearly BRAF-specific modifier than the primary `feature_009` signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_022 == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `feature_022 == 0`.
Among EGFR-dependent lines, the subgroup with `feature_022 == 1`, `feature_011 == brain`, and `feature_016 >= 1.088` has the most negative `dependency_EGFR` scores.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_EGFR among feature_022 == 1 versus feature_022 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_022 == 1` lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6010 versus -0.2524 in other lines, effect -0.3486.

analyses.code: Systematic heterogeneity search over strongest EGFR predicates; tested the triple subgroup feature_022 == 1, feature_011 == brain, feature_016 >= 1.088.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_022 == 1`, `feature_011 == brain`, `feature_016 >= 1.088` subgroup had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6770 versus -0.2788 in all other lines (n=89), effect -0.3983. This refined subgroup is deeper than the main effect but is still primarily anchored by `feature_022 == 1`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_024 == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `feature_024 == 0`.
The subgroup with `feature_024 == 1` and `feature_010 == her2_enriched` has especially negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores compared with all other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_ERBB2 among feature_024 == 1 versus feature_024 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_024 == 1` lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.6856 versus -0.2535 in other lines, effect -0.4321.

analyses.code: Subgroup and interaction screen over ERBB2 modifiers; tested feature_024 == 1 and feature_010 == her2_enriched.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_024 == 1` and `feature_010 == her2_enriched` subgroup had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.7525 versus -0.2944 in all other lines (n=142), effect -0.4581. The two-way interaction coefficient was -0.0671, indicating additional negative dependency beyond the additive feature effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_014 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `feature_014 == 0`.
The subgroup with `feature_014 == 1` and `feature_016 >= 1.088` has especially negative `dependency_KRAS` scores compared with all other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_KRAS among feature_014 == 1 versus feature_014 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_014 == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.5594 versus -0.2520 in other lines, effect -0.3075.

analyses.code: Systematic KRAS subgroup screen; tested feature_014 == 1 and feature_016 >= 1.088.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_014 == 1` and `feature_016 >= 1.088` subgroup had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.6092 versus -0.3177 in all other lines (n=1216), effect -0.2915. Interaction screening found a modest independent negative interaction for `feature_016 >= 1.088` with `feature_020 == 1` (coefficient -0.0596, p=0.00029), but the best-supported KRAS dependency remained anchored by `feature_014 == 1`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_027 == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `feature_027 == 0`.
The subgroup with `feature_027 == 1` and `feature_007 <= -1.097` has especially negative `dependency_RIT1` scores compared with all other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_RIT1 among feature_027 == 1 versus feature_027 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_027 == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.3527 versus -0.2572 in other lines, effect -0.0955.

analyses.code: Systematic RIT1 subgroup screen over strongest predicates; tested feature_027 == 1 and feature_007 <= -1.097.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_027 == 1` and `feature_007 <= -1.097` subgroup had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.3857 versus -0.2648 in all other lines (n=460), effect -0.1209. The corresponding interaction term was not significant, so this appears to be a deeper descriptive subgroup rather than a robust independent interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_011 == colorectal` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.

The complete subgroup with `feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_035 == 1`, and `feature_028 >= 1.427` has the strongest negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores; absence of any of these predicates suppresses the dependency.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_KIF18A among feature_011 == colorectal versus all other lines.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == colorectal` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.7204 versus -0.2524 in other lines, effect -0.4680.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over all KIF18A modifiers; tested the triple subgroup feature_011 == colorectal, feature_035 == 1, feature_028 >= 1.427.
analyses.result_summary: The triple subgroup `feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_035 == 1`, and `feature_028 >= 1.427` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.1961 versus -0.2678 in all other lines (n=2101), effect -0.9282.

analyses.code: Two-way interaction tests among the complete KIF18A subgroup predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest interaction terms supported the complete subgroup definition: `feature_011 == colorectal` x `feature_035 == 1` coefficient -0.6822, `feature_011 == colorectal` x `feature_028 >= 1.427` coefficient -0.6050, and `feature_028 >= 1.427` x `feature_035 == 1` coefficient -0.4952.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_016 >= 1.088` have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than cell lines with lower `feature_016`.
The subgroup with `feature_016 >= 1.088` and `feature_026 >= 1.153` has especially negative `dependency_TMED10` scores compared with all other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_TMED10 among feature_016 >= 1.088 versus lower feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 1.088` lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2906 versus -0.2499 in other lines, effect -0.0407.

analyses.code: Systematic TMED10 heterogeneity search; tested feature_016 >= 1.088 and feature_026 >= 1.153.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_016 >= 1.088` and `feature_026 >= 1.153` subgroup had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.3424 versus -0.2531 in all other lines (n=490), effect -0.0893. The interaction coefficient was -0.0369, suggesting high `feature_026` amplifies the high-`feature_016` dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_016 >= 1.088` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than cell lines with lower `feature_016`.
The subgroup with `feature_016 >= 1.088` and `feature_011 == ovarian` has especially negative `dependency_DHX9` scores compared with all other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_DHX9 among feature_016 >= 1.088 versus lower feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 1.088` lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3727 versus -0.3293 in other lines, effect -0.0433.

analyses.code: Systematic DHX9 heterogeneity search; tested feature_016 >= 1.088 and feature_011 == ovarian.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_016 >= 1.088` and `feature_011 == ovarian` subgroup had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.4183 versus -0.3329 in all other lines (n=435), effect -0.0854. The two-way interaction coefficient was -0.0424.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_016 >= 1.088` have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than cell lines with lower `feature_016`.
The subgroup with `feature_016 >= 1.088` and `feature_026 >= 1.153` has especially negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores compared with all other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_POLR2A among feature_016 >= 1.088 versus lower feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 1.088` lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.5281 versus -0.4070 in other lines, effect -0.1211.

analyses.code: Systematic POLR2A heterogeneity search; tested feature_016 >= 1.088 and feature_026 >= 1.153.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_016 >= 1.088` and `feature_026 >= 1.153` subgroup had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.5552 versus -0.4177 in all other lines (n=490), effect -0.1375. Pairwise interaction terms were weaker than the strong `feature_016` main effect, so this is best interpreted as high-`feature_016` dependency with a descriptive high-`feature_026` enrichment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_003 >= 1.003` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with lower `feature_003`.
The subgroup with `feature_003 >= 1.003`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_023 >= 6.33` has especially negative `dependency_RPL11` scores compared with all other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference test: mean dependency_RPL11 among feature_003 >= 1.003 versus lower feature_003.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_003 >= 1.003` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3831 versus -0.2573 in other lines, effect -0.1257.

analyses.code: Systematic RPL11 subgroup and interaction search; tested feature_003 >= 1.003, feature_016 >= 0.964, and feature_023 >= 6.33.
analyses.result_summary: The triple subgroup `feature_003 >= 1.003`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_023 >= 6.33` had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.4465 versus -0.2683 in all other lines (n=463), effect -0.1783. A related interaction between `feature_003 >= 0.428` and `feature_023 >= 6.33` had coefficient -0.0405.
